gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (16/183)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (16/183)
text: إِنَّ الْقَصْدَ أَمْرٌ يُحِبُّهُ اللَّهُ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ وَ إِنَّ السَّرَفَ أَمْرٌ يُبْغِضُهُ اللَّهُ حَتَّى طَرْحَكَ النَّوَاةَ فَإِنَّهَا تَصْلُحُ لِلشَّيْ‏ءِ وَ حَتَّى صَبَّكَ فَضْلَ شَرَابِكَ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from Salih Bin Al Sandy, from ja’far Bin Bashir, from Dawood Al Raqqy,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘The moderation is a matter which is Beloved to Allah<sup>azwj</sup> Mighty and Majestic, and the extravagance is a matter Hated by Allah<sup>azwj</sup> to the extent of the date-stone, It applies even to one’s throwing the stone inside the piece of a date; it can be useful for something, and to throwing the leftover of what you drink.
en.sarwar: 2. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from Salih ibn al-Sindiy from Ja’far ibn Bashir from Dawud al-Riqqiy who has said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said that moderation is a matter that Allah, the Most Majestic, the Most Glorious, loves, and extravagance is a thing that Allah hates. It applies even to one’s throwing the stone inside the piece of a date; it can be useful for something, and to throwing the leftover of what you drink.’”
